events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We are asked to assess a scenario: missing load reactor on long motor leads in a VFDs & motor controls installation. Work stage: rough-complete. Condition: missing load reactor. This is a defect. According to NEC 430, and manufacturer manuals, for long motor leads (typically >100 ft, but depends on manufacturer), a load reactor is often recommended or required to protect the motor from voltage spikes due to reflected waves in VFD applications. Missing it is a non-compliant or defective condition. It poses risk of motor insulation breakdown, leading to failure. So decision: fail. Risk: high or critical? Likely high because it can lead to motor damage but may not be immediate safety hazard. Work stage: rough-complete (as given). Component conditions: at least "installed-defective" (missing is defect) and maybe "non-compliant". Percent complete: not fully done, but rough-complete; maybe 90%? but missing component, so maybe estimate 80%? Value: not needed. Confidence: high. Findings: "Missing load reactor on long motor leads" and maybe "Risk of motor insulation damage due to reflected wave voltage spikes". Actions: "Install appropriate load reactor per manufacturer specifications". Rationale: brief. References: NEC 430 (maybe 430.122 for VFDs? Actually, NEC doesn't explicitly require load reactors, but manufacturer instructions are part of listing and labeling, so per NEC 110.3(B) equipment must be installed per manufacturer instructions, which may mandate load reactor for long leads. So reference: NEC 110.3(B), manufacturer installation manual. So output JSON.

We need exactly the JSON object. So construct it.

{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 80,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0,
  "rate": 0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": ["Missing load reactor on long motor leads between VFD and motor"],
  "actions": ["Install the appropriate load reactor per VFD manufacturer installation instructions"],
  "rationale": "Long motor leads in VFD applications can cause reflected wave voltage spikes, damaging motor insulation. Manufacturer instructions require a load reactor for lead lengths exceeding a specified distance. Missing it is a non-compliant and defective installation.",
  "references": ["NEC 110.3(B)", "VFD manufacturer installation manual"]
}

percent_complete: rough-complete implies most work done, but missing component reduces. Estimate 80% because VFD and motor are likely installed, just missing reactor. Confidence high. No sound_source so empty string. event_time and rate 0. order empty. workable empty. That's it. Ensure valid JSON.
